text: I’ll inspect the fixture manifest and available bomly tooling first, then make the narrowest dependency changes and verify with the repo test target.

The repo has a single fixture Maven app under `fixtures/api-java`. I’m going to inspect the root build target and the Maven dependency declarations, then run the dependency scanner against the fixture.

The scan found 12 vulnerable packages. Most have fixed versions; two advisories are reported as having no upstream fixed version, so I’ll document those explicitly and avoid inventing a version-only fix.

I’m going to add Maven dependency management for the vulnerable transitive packages and bump the direct versions already centralized in properties where possible. I’ll align Logback classic with the fixed core version to avoid a mixed Logback runtime.
The POM is updated. Next I’m checking whether the selected patched versions still resolve and compile before running the full root test target.

Maven confirmed `mysql:mysql-connector-java:8.2.0` is not published under the old coordinates. I’m switching that direct dependency to the current `com.mysql:mysql-connector-j` coordinates while keeping the same patched version.
The coordinate issue is fixed. I’m rerunning compile now; if a patched library has an API break, I’ll adjust to the smallest compatible patched version that still remediates the advisory.

`cyclonedx-core-java` 11.0.1 resolves but removed/changed several APIs used by this 4.10 fixture. Since that version is required for the second CycloneDX advisory, I’m checking the replacement API surface and will make only the compatibility edits needed for compile/tests.

The CycloneDX changes are mechanical: factories moved under `org.cyclonedx.generators` / `org.cyclonedx.parsers`, and license expressions now use a typed `Expression`. I’m patching those call sites only.
The CycloneDX call sites are patched. I’m compiling again before moving on to the scanner and test pass.
Compile is green. I’m rescanning the fixture now to confirm which vulnerability findings are closed and what remains unfixed upstream.

The rescan now reports only two vulnerable packages, both classified `no_fix_upstream`. I’m checking the full dependency scope once so the documentation doesn’t miss a vulnerable test/development dependency.

The all-scope scan also found vulnerable test transitive dependencies through WireMock and MockServer. I’m going to remediate those with dependency-management pins rather than changing test frameworks, unless a direct upgrade is necessary for compatibility.

The vulnerable development packages are all transitives. I’m adding explicit test-transitive pins for Jetty 9.x, Netty, FileUpload, Handlebars, XMLUnit, Bouncy Castle, BeanUtils, and Rhino, then I’ll let Maven/tests prove compatibility.
The transitive pins are in place. I’m running dependency resolution/compile again; this should catch any non-existent version or incompatible test dependency before the full suite.
The patched test transitives resolve and compile. I’m rescanning all scopes to verify only the no-fix runtime advisories remain.

The second scan exposed a few newly visible findings after earlier pins: AssertJ direct, Jackson core/json-path through WireMock, and newer Bouncy Castle/Rhino fixes through MockServer. Jetty 9’s remaining `jetty-http` findings are the problematic ones: one is marked no-fix upstream, and the other requires Jetty 12, which is not a safe transitive override under WireMock 2.x.
I’m adding the remaining compatible pins: AssertJ 3.27.7, Jackson core 2.18.6, json-path 2.9.0, Bouncy Castle 1.84 for both bcprov/bcpkix, and Rhino 1.8.1.
Those remaining pins are in the POM. I’m compiling again, then I’ll rescan and run the required `make test`.

At this point the only fix-available finding left is `jetty-http` requiring Jetty 12 while `wiremock-jre8` is on Jetty 9. I’m going to verify the required test target with the compatible Jetty 9 patched line, and I’ll document that Jetty 12 is not a version-only safe fix for this WireMock 2.x dependency.
`make test` is running and has passed the early model tests; I’m waiting for the full Maven target to finish before writing the final status.
`make test` passed: 336 tests, no failures or errors. I’m now writing `FIXES.md` with one entry per vulnerable package and the action taken, including the three remaining no-version-fix cases.

Implemented the dependency remediation under `fixtures/api-java` and documented it in [FIXES.md](/tmp/bomly-study-e67268lj/codex-mcp-api-java-1-1783589636/repo/FIXES.md).

Changed [pom.xml](/tmp/bomly-study-e67268lj/codex-mcp-api-java-1-1783589636/repo/fixtures/api-java/pom.xml) to bump direct dependencies and add dependency-management pins for vulnerable transitives. Also made minimal CycloneDX API compatibility edits needed for `cyclonedx-core-java` `11.0.1`.

Verification:
- `make test` passed: 336 tests, 0 failures, 0 errors.
- Final bomly scan still reports remaining vulnerable packages where version remediation is not available or not compatible:
  - `io.pebbletemplates:pebble`: no fixed upstream version.
  - `com.fasterxml.jackson.core:jackson-databind`: one advisory has no fixed upstream version.
  - `org.eclipse.jetty:jetty-http` via `wiremock-jre8`: one advisory has no fixed upstream version; another requires Jetty 12, which is not a compatible version-only override for WireMock 2.x’s Jetty 9 stack.
